questions.q: The Chola Empire was a dynasty of which language region of India?
questions.explanation: The Cholas were one of the ancient Tamil dynasties of southern India.

questions.q: During which centuries did the medieval Chola Empire reach its height?
questions.explanation: The imperial Cholas were at their zenith from the 10th to 12th centuries CE.

questions.q: The Cholas are especially celebrated for their bronze statues of which kind?
options: Hindu deities such as the dancing Shiva (Nataraja)
questions.explanation: Chola bronzes, above all the Nataraja form of Shiva, are world-renowned masterpieces.

questions.q: The Cholas projected naval power across the seas to Sri Lanka and which other region?
questions.explanation: The Cholas mounted naval expeditions reaching Sri Lanka and Southeast Asia.

questions.q: The royal emblem of the Cholas was which animal?
questions.explanation: The Chola emblem was the tiger, distinguishing them from the Pandya fish and Chera bow.

questions.q: The Chola heartland lay in the fertile delta of which river?
questions.explanation: The Chola core territory was the rich Kaveri (Cauvery) delta in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: The Cholas are famed for building what kind of monumental structures?
questions.explanation: The Cholas were prolific builders of grand stone temples across the south.

questions.q: Which famous Chola temple at Thanjavur is a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara temple at Thanjavur, built by Rajaraja I, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: The Cholas were one of the three ancient crowned Tamil dynasties, along with the Pandyas and which other?
questions.explanation: The Cheras, Cholas, and Pandyas were the three crowned Tamil dynasties.

questions.q: The Cholas are praised for an advanced and well-organised system of what?
questions.explanation: The Cholas are celebrated for their efficient administration and village self-government.

questions.q: Who founded the imperial line of the medieval Cholas in the 9th century by capturing Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya Chola revived Chola fortunes around 850 CE by seizing Thanjavur, founding the imperial line.

questions.q: Which Chola emperor built the Brihadisvara temple at Thanjavur and expanded the empire around 1000 CE?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja Chola I built the great Brihadisvara temple and greatly enlarged the empire.

questions.q: Which Chola emperor led an expedition to the Ganges and naval campaigns against Srivijaya in Southeast Asia?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I marched to the Ganges and launched naval expeditions against Srivijaya.

questions.q: Rajendra I founded a new Chola capital to commemorate his Ganges expedition; what was it called?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I founded Gangaikonda Cholapuram, 'the city of the Chola who took the Ganges'.

questions.q: The signature Chola bronze depicting Shiva dancing within a ring of flames is known as which form?
questions.explanation: The Nataraja, Shiva as Lord of the Dance, is the most iconic Chola bronze.

questions.q: Chola village assemblies that governed local affairs are documented in inscriptions from which famous site near Kanchipuram?
questions.explanation: The Uttiramerur inscriptions detail the Chola system of elected village assemblies (sabha).

questions.q: Rajaraja I led a major naval conquest of which island kingdom to the south?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I conquered much of Sri Lanka, ending Anuradhapura's role as its capital.

questions.q: Which dynasty did the Cholas displace in the Tamil country when they rose to power under Aditya I and Parantaka I?
questions.explanation: Aditya I overthrew the Pallavas, and the Cholas supplanted them as the dominant Tamil power.

questions.q: The towering pyramidal tower over the sanctum of a Chola temple is known by which term?
questions.explanation: The vimana is the great tower over the sanctum; the Brihadisvara's vimana is especially renowned.

questions.q: Which later Chola emperor united the Chola and Eastern Chalukya lines and ruled from around 1070 CE?
questions.explanation: Kulottunga I, of mixed Chola and Eastern Chalukya descent, ruled a reunited realm from 1070.

questions.q: Rajendra I sent a famous diplomatic and trade mission to the court of which east Asian power?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I's reign saw Chola embassies to Song China, reflecting active maritime trade.

questions.q: The Brihadisvara temple at Thanjavur was completed around which year?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I's Brihadisvara (Rajarajeshvaram) temple was completed around 1010 CE.

questions.q: Rajendra I's naval campaign of around 1025 CE targeted the maritime empire of Srivijaya, then centred on which island region?
questions.explanation: The Srivijaya empire of Sumatra and the Malay region was struck by Rajendra I's overseas expedition.

questions.q: The Chola merchant guild that dominated overseas trade and is named in inscriptions across South and Southeast Asia was known as which?
questions.explanation: The Ainurruvar (the Five Hundred of Ayyavole) were a powerful Chola-era merchant guild trading across Asia.

questions.q: Rajaraja I's many conquests are recorded in the inscriptions on the walls of which temple?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara temple walls carry detailed inscriptions of Rajaraja I's reign and conquests.

questions.q: Which Chola king's reign is generally taken to mark the start of the imperial Cholas' decline, with the empire finally absorbed by the Pandyas in the 13th century?
questions.explanation: Rajendra III was the last Chola sovereign; by the mid-13th century the resurgent Pandyas absorbed the realm.

questions.q: The original name given by Rajaraja I to the great Thanjavur temple, after his own title, was which?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I named the temple Rajarajeshvaram after himself; it is now called Brihadisvara.

questions.q: Rajaraja I assumed which grand imperial title reflecting his conquests across the southern seas and lands?
options: Mummudi Chola (the Chola of three crowns)
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I took titles such as Mummudi Chola, claiming the crowns of the three Tamil dynasties.

questions.q: Rajendra I built a massive irrigation tank near his new capital; it was known as the 'liquid pillar of victory', or which lake?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I excavated the great Cholagangam tank at Gangaikonda Cholapuram, called a 'liquid pillar of victory'.

questions.q: The Eastern Chalukya marriage alliances that produced Kulottunga I linked the Cholas with rulers of which region?
questions.explanation: Kulottunga I descended from the Eastern Chalukyas of Vengi in the Andhra region, united with the Chola line by marriage.
